---
title: "Modelling Behaviour in Matching Pennies"
---

This site walks through fitting behavioural models to matching pennies data,
where a player repeatedly chooses Left or Right against an opponent and is
rewarded for matching (or mismatching) the opponent's choice.

We cover three notebooks:

1. **[Synthetic Data](notebooks/01_synthetic_data.qmd)** -- generate choice
   sequences from known strategies (biased, win-stay-lose-switch, perseverant)
   so we have ground-truth data to validate the models.
2. **[GLM-HMM](notebooks/02_glm_hmm.qmd)** -- fit a Generalized Linear Model
   with Hidden Markov Model states to recover latent behavioural strategies.
3. **[Q-Learning](notebooks/03_qlearning.qmd)** -- fit a reinforcement-learning
   model (Lee et al.) that tracks action values trial by trial.
